response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist software engineer with solid software-craftsmanship habits and some low-latency/high-throughput experience, but the role is a Principal-level hardware/performance architect for AI accelerators. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD work, or deep-learning training/inference optimization — the core required and preferred qualifications. The brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments and 'embedded' mention do not approach chip-level performance architecture. The experience domain is fundamentally different from what the role demands.
strengths: Deep, broad software engineering experience with strong TDD/CI/CD craftsmanship
Some low-latency, high-throughput systems work (microservices handling millions of req/s)
Meets a minimal education path (BS CS + 8+ years experience)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI accelerator, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD background

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator / GPU / deep-learning expertise
